mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls expanded by 916k in March 2021, beating expectations
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of out-of-sample observations, or if the Rank IC of PAYEMS changes versus 20-day returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 0.25 percent anchors the risk-free rate at the zero lower bound, compressing equity discount rates and inflating valuation multiples, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 0.07% as of March 2021 with forward guidance indicating no hikes through 2023
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns during periods of FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly positive compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or if the strategy Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and retail sales confirms a broad-based demand recovery spanning goods production and consumer spending, reducing recession probability and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales and industrial output both rebounded above pre-pandemic levels by early 2021
failure_condition: If joint positive changes in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a regression against single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, rapid declines in the unemployment rate signal faster-than-expected labor market repair, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines more than 0.5 percentage points over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 6.7% in December 2020 to 6.0% in March 2021
failure_condition: If in post-shock recovery periods, UNRATE declines exceeding 0.5pp coincide with negative 20-day S&P returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,400 thousand units indicate robust residential construction activity and upstream multiplier effects across materials, labor, and credit markets, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts printed 1,421k in February 2021, reflecting strong housing demand
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns following months with HOUST above 1400 are negative in more than 45% of observations, or if the hit rate of positive returns falls below 55% over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production captures positive supply-side momentum and factory operating leverage, which leads to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial Production index at 105.583 in March 2021, recovering sharply from pandemic lows
failure_condition: If positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P returns in more than 50% of observations, or if the directional accuracy of INDPRO acceleration falls below 52% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated unemployment and a near-zero federal funds rate creates a persistent monetary stimulus overhang that compresses risk premiums and supports S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed maintained 0-0.25% target in March 2021 while unemployment remained elevated at 6.0%
failure_condition: If periods with UNRATE above 5% and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% do not produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the joint signal's information coefficient is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation signals rising input costs and impending monetary tightening that compresses corporate profit margins and equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI accelerated to 2.6% year-over-year in March 2021, the fastest pace since 2018
failure_condition: If months with CPIAUCSL 3-month change above 1.5% are followed by negative 20-day S&P returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or if the signal's directional accuracy is not statistically significant (binomial p > 0.10).
